Regime-aware global liquid ETF allocation

RiskDesk

A research cockpit that turns a global liquid ETF universe into momentum, regime-aware risk allocation, and explainable machine-learning portfolios. Reference metadata is separated from illustrative model output so every result has a visible data boundary.

Advanced Model Lab

Three distinct research engines use the same ETF universe. Select a model to see its inputs, decision chain, and illustrative allocation output.

Research mode

Cross-Sectional Momentum

Ranks ETFs by 12-month return while skipping the most recent month, selects leaders, then reduces the influence of volatile holdings. This is the transparent baseline.

Rank12-1 month relative momentum
Control60-day realized volatility
AllocateInverse-vol weights, 10% target
V1 EquationsMi,t = Pi,t-21 / Pi,t-252 - 1 12-1 momentumσi,t = √252 · sd(ri,t-59:t) annualized 60-day volatilityw̃i,t = (1 / σi,t) / Σj∈Top3(1 / σj,t)wi,t = min(2, 0.10 / σp,t) · w̃i,t 10% target volatility
Calculating public-data rankingLatest snapshot

Regime-Aware Risk Allocation

A genuine hidden Markov model estimates market state, GARCH forecasts conditional volatility, and constrained mean-variance optimization converts forecasts into weights.

DetectLow/high-volatility state probability
ForecastGARCH(1,1) asset risk
OptimizeLong-only, capped MVO weights
V2 Equationszt ∈ {Low, High},   P(zt=j | zt-1=i) = aijrt | zt=k ~ N(μk, σk2) Gaussian HMM emissionhi,t = ωi + αiri,t-12 + βihi,t-1 GARCH(1,1)maxw [ μ′w - (λ(zt)/2)w′Σw ]Σwi=1,   0≤wi≤0.35 long-only capped allocation
Loading V2 backtestHMM / GARCH / MVO

Explainable ML Alpha

A regularized gradient-boosted decision-stump model predicts next-month ETF returns from momentum, volatility, trend and drawdown features. Additive tree contributions explain each forecast.

FeaturesEight price and risk features
PredictExpanding walk-forward training
ExplainAdditive tree contribution audit
V3 Equationsxi,t = [Mom1M, Mom3M, Mom6M, Mom12-1, Vol20, Vol60, MA50Gap, DD6M]FM(x) = ȳ + η Σm=1M hm(x) boosted predictionhm = arg minh Σn[yn-Fm-1(xn)-h(xn)]2 + λ‖h‖2F(x) = Base + Σj φj(x) additive feature contribution
Loading V3 backtestBoosted trees
Data status: V1, V2 and V3 now use real adjusted-price histories and walk-forward decisions. V2 implements a two-state Gaussian HMM, GARCH(1,1) likelihood search and capped MVO. V3 uses a locally implemented boosted-tree model, not the external XGBoost package; its attribution is additive tree contribution, not library TreeSHAP.

Growth of 100

V1, V2 and V3 against the passive SPY benchmark.

V1 MomentumV2 HMM-GARCH-MVOV3 Boosted TreesSPY
Methodology

Signal: 12-1 month cross-sectional momentum. Selection: top three positive ETFs. Weighting: inverse 60-day volatility, scaled to a 10% annual target with a 2x cap. Sharpe assumes a zero risk-free rate.

Known limitation: the universe contains today's surviving ETFs, so delisted funds are not represented. This may create survivorship bias.

Latest Rebalance
Research WorkflowActual project status
01 / Intake Idea Capture

Log the market thesis, eligible ETFs, target holding period, and the reason the opportunity should exist.

Owner: ResearchStatus: Complete
02 / Research Hypothesis Review

Translate the idea into measurable signals, expected failure modes, and clear approval criteria.

Check: BiasStatus: Complete
03 / Data Data Validation

Confirm prices, dividends, benchmark history, missing values, splits, and survivorship assumptions.

Source: Public SnapshotStatus: Complete
04 / Signal Signal Build

Generate ranking, allocation, entry, exit, and cooldown rules with turnover controls attached.

Output: V1–V3 + 12 RulesStatus: Complete
05 / Test Backtest & Stress

Run walk-forward testing, recession windows, rate shocks, liquidity cuts, and transaction-cost sensitivity.

Walk-forward: CompleteRobustness: Pending
06 / Risk Risk Limits

Set VaR budget, drawdown stop, factor caps, correlation rules, and escalation thresholds.

Core metrics: CompleteLimits: Pending
07 / Launch Paper Trading

Monitor slippage, trade frequency, drift, and signal decay before allowing production capital.

Mode: Not startedStatus: Pending
08 / Live Production Monitor

Track live PnL, limit usage, alerts, execution quality, and monthly model-retirement decisions.

Hosting: PendingStatus: Not live
This editable prototype is for research and risk-management interface design only. It is not investment advice.
